info.title: Mahi (Manta Auth Cache) API
info.description: Mahi - Manta Auth Cache. Redis-backed auth cache exposing UFDS-sourced account/user/role/policy lookups plus AWS SigV4/STS/IAM endpoints.

get.summary: Look up an account by login (`?login=` or legacy alias `?account=`).

parameters.name: login
parameters.description: Account login. Upstream also accepts the historical alias `account=` for the same value.

get.summary: Look up an account by UUID. Returns `AuthInfo` populated with account
Resolve a principal by AWS access key id. Falls back to UFDS for

post.summary: Verify an AWS SigV4 signature over the original client request.
post.description: The original method / URL are delivered in the query string. The service implementation must inspect the incoming request's headers directly (via `RequestContext::request`); the request body is opaque here.

parameters.name: method
parameters.description: HTTP method of the original (to-be-verified) request.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: URL (path + query) of the original (to-be-verified) request.

post.summary: Create a new IAM role. Writes Redis synchronously, UFDS asynchronously.

delete.summary: Delete a role. Writes Redis synchronously, UFDS asynchronously.
Detach a policy from a role. All identifiers come from the query

get.summary: Get a single role by name.
Retrieve a specific role policy document (as a JSON-encoded string).
List policies attached to a role. Note the lowercase `maxitems` query
get.description: parameter (an upstream quirk; `maxItems` is accepted as an alias).

parameters.name: maxitems
parameters.description: Maximum number of policy names to return. Upstream is `maxitems` (lowercase) in the handler; this alias accepts the typo-free spelling for forward compatibility.

parameters.name: maxItems
parameters.description: Upstream default is 100; server caps at 1000.

post.summary: Attach a policy to a role.

get.summary: Bulk lookup of all known accounts. Response is a map keyed by account
Resolve a set of UUIDs to their names/logins. The response is a map
get.description: keyed by UUID, not an array.

parameters.name: uuid
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of UUIDs to resolve.

get.summary: Health check. Returns 204 on success; 503 if Redis is unavailable or
get.description: the replicator is not caught up.

get.summary: List members of a role identified by `(account, role-name)`. The
get.description: returned `AuthInfo.role` field is populated for this endpoint.

parameters.name: role
parameters.description: Role name. Upstream also accepts the alias `name=`.

post.summary: Mint MSAR-prefixed temporary credentials after validating the trust
Return caller-identity information. The wire response is XML with
post.description: `Content-Type: text/xml`, so this endpoint uses `Response<Body>` and relies on a Phase-2b OpenAPI spec patch to declare the correct content type and a `string` schema.

post.summary: Mint MSTS-prefixed temporary credentials for the caller.

get.summary: Look up a sub-user by (account-login, sub-user-login).
get.description: When `fallback=true` (the upstream default) and the sub-user is missing, the handler returns the account-only `AuthInfo` with `user=None` and an empty `roles` map.

parameters.name: fallback
parameters.description: When `true`, missing sub-users are swallowed and the account-only `AuthInfo` (no `user`, empty `roles`) is returned with HTTP 200. Defaults to `true` upstream. Accepts boolean `true`/`false` or the strings `"true"`/`"false"`.

get.summary: Look up a sub-user by UUID. Also loads the owning account.
Resolve a set of names to their UUIDs within an account. Returns
get.description: `{account}` alone when no `name=` query is supplied.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of names, or `None` for "no names".

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Type of object to look up.

AccessKeySecret.description:
Secret material stored alongside an access key id.

Lives under `user.accesskeys[keyId]`. For temporary credentials, the `sessionToken`, `expiration`, `principalUuid`, and `assumedRole` fields are populated; for permanent credentials only `secret` and `type` are present.

assumedRole.description: Assumed-role metadata for MSAR-prefixed temporary credentials.

principalUuid.description: Uuid of the principal (user) the temporary credential belongs to.

sessionToken.description: Opaque session-token blob returned to clients using STS credentials.

Account.description:
Redis-backed account blob (UFDS passthrough).

The concrete set of fields on accounts varies by deployment and UFDS schema. We model the commonly-observed fields explicitly and capture any extras via `#[serde(flatten)]` into a catch-all map.

isOperator.description: Set by `redislib.getAccount`. Camel-cased on the wire (UFDS bool).

key_info.description: Per-fingerprint attestation metadata (`attested`, `pin`, `touch`) replicated from UFDS `sdckey` entries. Keyed by the same fingerprint as `keys`.

keys.description: Key fingerprints -> key blobs. Shape is deployment-specific.

roles.description: Role uuids attached at the account level.

DurationSeconds.description: Desired lifetime in seconds. Upstream validates the range 900..=43200.

AssumeRoleResponse.description:
Outer response envelope returned by `POST /sts/assume-role`.

Mahi emulates AWS's XML envelope with a JSON object of the same shape, so callers must navigate two nested `AssumeRoleResponse` / `AssumeRoleResult` keys.

AssumedRole.description: Assumed-role context attached to temporary credentials.

AuthInfo.description:
Auth payload returned by most lookup endpoints.

Mahi builds this up progressively as `req.auth` and sends it back verbatim. `user` is optional because several routes (and the `fallback=true` branch of `GET /users`) only populate `account` + `roles`. `role` is only populated by `GET /roles`.

roles.description: Roles map keyed by role uuid.

AwsAuthResult.description:
Result of `GET /aws-auth/:accesskeyid`.

Contains the full principal context plus, when applicable, assumed-role / temporary-credential metadata.

Caller.description: The `caller` object every STS endpoint expects in its body.

CallerAccount.description: Account identifier attached to STS calls.

CallerUser.description: Optional sub-user identifier attached to STS calls.

path.description: IAM path. Upstream defaults to `"/"` when absent.

CredentialType.description:
Credential type for access keys.

Wire format values are `"permanent"` and `"temporary"`. Temporary credentials are produced by the STS endpoints (MSAR/MSTS prefixes).

oneOf.description: Catch-all for credential types added after this client was compiled.

GetCallerIdentityRequest.description:
Request body for `POST /sts/get-caller-identity`.

The response is emitted as raw XML with `Content-Type: text/xml`; the trait therefore uses `Result<Response<Body>, HttpError>` for this endpoint and a Phase-2b spec patch reshapes the response schema accordingly.

DurationSeconds.description: Desired lifetime in seconds. Upstream validates 900..=129600.

IamRole.description: IAM role object (PascalCase on the wire).

ListRolesResponse.description:
Response for `GET /iam/list-roles`.

**Mixed casing** at the top level: `roles` is lowercase, `IsTruncated` and `Marker` are PascalCase. Apply field-level renames rather than a struct `rename_all`.

LookupEntry.description: Entry value for `GET /lookup` responses (map keyed by account uuid).

MantaPolicy.description: Mahi-native policy payload attached to a role via `PutRolePolicy`.

name.description: Single name or array of names.

NameToUuidResponse.description:
Response for `GET /uuids` and `POST /getUuid`.

When no `name=` was supplied, only `account` is populated. Otherwise `uuids` contains a map of `name -> uuid` for each requested name.

ObjectType.description:
Object types accepted by `GET /uuids?type=`.

The set is asserted in upstream `redislib.getUuid` and is lowercase on the wire.

ObjectTypeTag.description: Internal `type` field stored on every Redis blob. Mahi may grow additional object types over time, so include a catch-all `Unknown` variant.

oneOf.description: Catch-all for object types added after this client was compiled.

PolicyEntry.description: Policy entry attached to an assumed role.

permissionPolicies.description: Policy metadata attached for STS evaluation. Shape is internal. Wire name is camelCase (`permissionPolicies`); it is the only camelCase field on this otherwise-lowercase Redis blob.

policies.description: Policy uuids attached to this role.

SigV4VerifyResult.description:
Result of `POST /aws-verify` on a valid signature.

The handler always responds with `valid: true` on success (invalid signatures come back as HTTP errors).

assumedRole.description: Assumed-role metadata (present only for MSAR temporary credentials).

signingKey.description: Derived signing key. Upstream JSON-encodes a Node `Buffer`, so the on-the-wire shape is `{"type":"Buffer","data":[...]}`. Phase 5 should confirm whether any caller actually consumes this; we keep it opaque for now.

StringOrVec.description: Accepts either a single value or a JSON array of values (both forms are used by the deprecated `POST /getUuid` and `POST /getName` endpoints).

accesskeys.description: Access keys keyed by access-key id. Values contain secret material.

key_info.description: Per-fingerprint attestation metadata (`attested`, `pin`, `touch`) replicated from UFDS `sdckey` entries. Keyed by the same fingerprint as `keys`.

keys.description:
SSH public-key fingerprints -> key blobs, replicated from UFDS `sdckey` entries attached to the sub-user. Shape mirrors [`Account::keys`]: the blob is deployment-specific (PEM SubjectPublicKeyInfo on the Triton deployments we've seen, `ssh-rsa AAAA...` on some others) and verifiers should parse both.

Must be modeled explicitly here — if it lived only in `#[serde(flatten)] extra`, Progenitor's regenerated client type would silently drop it at the wire boundary, since the generated client has no catch-all.

roles.description: Role uuids attached at the user level.

uuid.description: Single uuid or array of uuids. Kept as `String` here because the body may carry either a bare string or a JSON array of strings; UUID parsing happens in the service layer so we can surface a clean 400 for malformed input.
